title: Buttondown vs Constant Contact (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Buttondown and Constant Contact. Find the best email marketing tool for your needs with our detailed feature and pricing analysis.
h1: Buttondown vs Constant Contact: Detailed Comparison
intro: Buttondown and Constant Contact serve different segments of the email marketing market. Buttondown caters to developers and technical writers seeking a markdown-first, minimalist approach, while Constant Contact targets nonprofits and local businesses needing comprehensive marketing features. This comparison will help you determine which platform aligns best with your specific needs.

comparison_table.tool_b: Event management, SMS, e-commerce, social ads

choose_a_if: You want a free tier to get started without commitment
You prefer writing in markdown or code-friendly environments
You need API access for custom integrations and development
choose_b_if: You require advanced features like SMS marketing and event management
You need e-commerce integration for selling products
You prefer drag-and-drop template builders for visual design

faq.q: Is Buttondown better than Constant Contact?
faq.a: It depends on your use case. Buttondown excels for technical users and writers who value simplicity and markdown support, boasting higher customer ratings (4.8 G2, 4.7 Trustpilot). Constant Contact offers more marketing features and integrations, making it better for businesses needing SMS, events, and e-commerce functionality, despite lower ratings.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Buttondown or Constant Contact?
faq.a: Buttondown is more affordable overall—it offers a free tier for up to 100 subscribers and starts at $9/month for paid plans. Constant Contact has no free tier and begins at $12/month. For teams starting out, Buttondown provides better value, especially if you're building a small subscriber base.

faq.q: Can I switch from Constant Contact to Buttondown?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms support import/export functionality. Buttondown explicitly lists import/export as a key feature, making migration straightforward. You can export your subscriber list and campaigns from Constant Contact and import them into Buttondown, though you may need to redesign templates since the writing styles differ significantly.
